task_completion.description: Open the MergeTask project in Eclipse IDE
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE window is open with the 'MergeTask' project visible in the Project Explorer or Package Explorer.

task_completion.description: Open the target file DataProcessor.java in the Eclipse editor
task_completion.visual_evidence: An editor tab named 'DataProcessor.java' is open and active in Eclipse.

task_completion.description: Merge the required methods from v1 and v2 into DataProcessor.java
task_completion.visual_evidence: The visible code in DataProcessor.java shows the class 'DataProcessor' in package 'com.acme.util' and contains multiple merged methods such as parseCSVLine, computeStatistics, normalizeWhitespace, transformToMap, validateEmail, or sanitizeHTML.

task_completion.description: Ensure the merged DataProcessor.java compiles without errors
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are no red squiggly error markers under the code in the DataProcessor.java editor, and the Eclipse Problems view (if visible) shows no Java compilation errors for this file.

task_completion.description: Run and pass the DataProcessorTest.java JUnit test
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse JUnit view is visible, displaying a green progress bar and indicating that all tests in 'DataProcessorTest' have passed (e.g., 0 Errors, 0 Failures).

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Eclipse IDE (editor, menus, views) rather than relying solely on terminal commands like vim, nano, or sed to edit files.

integrity.description: The JUnit tests were genuinely executed within Eclipse
integrity.visual_evidence: The JUnit results are shown in the native Eclipse JUnit view with the characteristic green bar and test tree, not just printed text in a terminal or a manually created text file.

integrity.description: The agent manually merged the files or used Eclipse's Compare Editor
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of editing within Eclipse, such as using the Compare Editor, copying/pasting between tabs, or typing in the editor, rather than running a pre-written external script to generate the merged file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The final merged `DataProcessor.java` must be in package `com.acme.util` and contain the following 6 methods: `parseCSVLine` (from v1, containing `if (line == null)`), `computeStatistics` (from v2, containing `.parallelStream()`), `normalizeWhitespace` (from v1, containing `\p{Zs}`), `transformToMap` (from v2), `validateEmail` (from v2), and `sanitizeHTML` (from v1, containing `<[^>]*>`). Successful completion requires the Eclipse JUnit view to show a green bar for `DataProcessorTest`.
